author: Even Rouault
message:
som: avoid cppcheck warning

should fix https://github.com/OSGeo/PROJ/actions/runs/27851095459/job/82430145568
```
/home/runner/work/PROJ/PROJ/src/projections/som.cpp:227,style,knownConditionTrueFalse,Condition 'lam<=72.0001' is always false
```

This warning is a false positive but iteration condition based on
floating-point variables is considered a bad practice.

author: Even Rouault
message:
Database: update to EPSG 12.057

- NATRF2022 updates: the CRS is renamed to 'NATRF2022e2020'
- IGb20 is deprecated
- SIRGAS-Chile updates
- JGD2024 updates

3 issues reported to EPSG and worked around in this commit:

* transformation 11485 'ITRF2020 to NATRF2022e2020 (1)' uses millimetre/year
  for parameters "Rate of change of X axis translation" and
  "Rate of change of Y axis translation", but metre/year for
  "Rate of change of Z axis translation". Values of those parameters are zero
  so that doesn't make much of a practical difference, but I assume this
  is unwanted as this is the only Helmert transformation in the dataset
  where the unit is not identical for all X, Y, Z axis

* Transformation 10786 'ITRF2020-u2023 to IGb20 (1)' is non deprecated, but
  its source CRS is 10779 'ITRF2020-u2023' which is deprecated .This is the
  only record in the dataset where a non-deprecated Helmert transformation
  refers to a deprecated source CRS. Its target CRS 10786 'IGb20' is also deprecated

* Similarly, Transformation 10787 'IGS20 to IGb20 (1)' is non deprecated, but
  its target CRS is 10779 10786 'IGb20'  which is deprecated

author: Even Rouault
message:
tinshift: support TIN GeoPackage files

Such files are equivalent to already supported TIN JSON files, but scale
better for arbitrarily large triangulations, in particular for
network-based access.

The format is defined in source/specifications/tin_gpkg.rst and the
https://github.com/OSGeo/PROJ-data/blob/master/grid_tools/tin_json_to_gpkg_tin.py
Python script may be used o convert an existing TIN JSON into a TIN
GeoPackage.

author: Greg Troxel
message:
install: Explain dependencies more

Point out vendored code and build-time downloading that most packagers
will want to avoid.

Explain the test program compilation plan more precisely.

Note that an outdated vendored version (from 2020-08) of nlohmann/json
may be used and how to avoid it.

author: Greg Troxel
message:
install: Shorten autotools history

9.0 was over 4 years ago.  The previous use of autotools is now only
of historical interest.  Simply say that the build system is CMake,
with a brief historical comment.

author: Greg Troxel
message:
install.rst: Say that the file covers building from source

It did, but the intro said it only addressed using package.

Note that the source build sections variously describe release
tarballs and git, and hint that either is ok.

Co-authored-by: Kristian Evers <kristian@evers.dev>
